teaching_card.title: Parallel Structure (Medium)
teaching_card.body: Items joined in a list or by 'and'/'or' should share the same grammatical form. If two items are -ing forms, the third must be too.
key_points: Match the form of items in a series.
-ing with -ing; nouns with nouns; to-verb with to-verb.
teaching_card.worked_example: She likes hiking, biking, and swimming (not 'to swim').

screens.title: Same form in a series
screens.body: When items are joined in a list or by and/or, they must share the same grammatical form: all -ing, all nouns, or all to-verbs. A mismatch stands out as the error.
key_points: -ing with -ing, noun with noun, to-verb with to-verb.
Find the item whose form doesn't match the others.

screens.title: Also for paired connectors
screens.body: Parallelism applies to paired structures too — 'not only … but also,' 'either … or,' 'both … and.' The words after each half should line up in form.
key_points: Make the parts after 'either/or' and 'not only/but also' match.
Comparisons ('more X than Y') should be parallel too.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: 'She likes hiking, biking, and to swim' breaks the pattern — two -ing words then a to-verb. Make the third match: 'hiking, biking, and swimming.'
screens.worked_example: to read, to write, and to edit (all to-verbs), not '…editing.'
